introSection: Falcon's Flight is a roller coaster manufactured by Swiss company Intamin located at Six Flags Qiddiya City in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. It is the world's fastest, tallest, and longest roller coaster. Portions of the coaster also go over the Qiddiya circuit, which is scheduled to host the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix from the 2024 season onward.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The first track piece was installed in April 2023.

The train was revealed in November 2023 at the IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA, along with other details. Intamin coined the term "Exa" to describe this coaster and others over 600 feet (183 metres) tall.

The airtime hill was topped off in May 2024.

The ride began testing by July 2025.

On December 31, 2025, Falcon's Flight, and the rest of the park opened to the public.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The height difference from the highest point to the lowest point of the coaster is approximately, as Falcon's Flight dives off a cliff. The highest point off the ground the coaster goes when the ride traverses a camelback.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Falcon's Flight has six trains, each seating 14 riders.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Ride page on the park's website

fullText:
Falcon's Flight is a roller coaster manufactured by Swiss company Intamin located at Six Flags Qiddiya City in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. It is the world's fastest, tallest, and longest roller coaster. Portions of the coaster also go over the Qiddiya circuit, which is scheduled to host the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix from the 2024 season onward.

The first track piece was installed in April 2023.

The train was revealed in November 2023 at the IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA, along with other details. Intamin coined the term "Exa" to describe this coaster and others over 600 feet (183 metres) tall.

The airtime hill was topped off in May 2024.

The ride began testing by July 2025.

On December 31, 2025, Falcon's Flight, and the rest of the park opened to the public.

The height difference from the highest point to the lowest point of the coaster is approximately, as Falcon's Flight dives off a cliff. The highest point off the ground the coaster goes when the ride traverses a camelback.

Falcon's Flight has six trains, each seating 14 riders.


 * Ride page on the park's website
